Quaternion¶

Quaternion class — single quaternion value type.

class quat.core.Quaternion(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: object

__init__(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Construct a Quaternion.

Example

>>> q = Quaternion(1, 2, 3, 4)       # (r, i, j, k)
>>> q = Quaternion(5.0)               # real scalar
>>> q = Quaternion(1+2j)              # from complex
>>> q = Quaternion(q2)                # copy
>>> q = Quaternion(np.array([1,2,3,4]))  # from ndarray
>>> q = Quaternion(scalar=1, vector=(2,3,4))  # keyword args
Return type:

None

classmethod zero()[source]¶
Return type:

Quaternion

classmethod one_q()[source]¶
Return type:

Quaternion

classmethod from_axis_angle(axis, angle)[source]¶

Construct a unit quaternion from an axis-angle rotation.

Example

>>> q = Quaternion.from_axis_angle((0, 0, 1), np.pi / 2)
>>> q  # 90° rotation about z-axis
Return type:

Quaternion

to_axis_angle()[source]¶

Convert unit quaternion to axis-angle representation.

Returns (axis, angle) where axis is a unit 3-vector and angle is in [0, pi] radians.

Example

>>> q = Quaternion.from_axis_angle((0, 0, 1), 1.2)
>>> axis, angle = q.to_axis_angle()
>>> abs(np.linalg.norm(axis) - 1) < 1e-10
True
>>> abs(angle - 1.2) < 1e-10
True
Return type:

Tuple[ndarray, float]

classmethod from_euler(angles, seq='zyx', intrinsic=True)[source]¶

Construct a quaternion from Euler angles.

Parameters:

  • angles – (roll, pitch, yaw) or (phi, theta, psi) in radians.

  • seq (str) – Rotation sequence, e.g. 'zyx', 'xyz', 'zxz'.

  • intrinsic (bool) – If True (default), rotate about moving axes; if False, rotate about fixed axes.

Returns:

Unit quaternion representing the composed rotation.

Return type:

Quaternion

Example

>>> q = Quaternion.from_euler((0, 0, np.pi/2))
>>> q  # yaw 90° about z
Quaternion(0.707..., 0.0, 0.0, 0.707...)
to_euler(seq='zyx', intrinsic=True)[source]¶

Extract Euler angles from a unit quaternion.

Uses the rotation sequence seq with the specified convention.

Parameters:

  • seq (str) – Rotation sequence, e.g. 'zyx'.

  • intrinsic (bool) – Same convention as from_euler.

Returns:

ndarray of 3 angles in radians.

Return type:

ndarray

Example

>>> q = Quaternion.from_euler((0.1, 0.2, 0.3))
>>> angles = q.to_euler()
>>> np.allclose(angles, [0.1, 0.2, 0.3])
True
property r: float¶
property i: float¶
property j: float¶
property k: float¶
property real: float¶
property imag: ndarray¶
property components: Tuple[float, float, float, float]¶
property shape: Tuple¶
conjugate()[source]¶

Return the quaternion conjugate (r, -i, -j, -k).

Example

>>> q = Quaternion(1, 2, 3, 4)
>>> q.conjugate()
Quaternion(1.0, -2.0, -3.0, -4.0)
Return type:

Quaternion

norm()[source]¶

Return the Euclidean norm.

Example

>>> q = Quaternion(3, 4, 0, 0)
>>> q.norm()
5.0
Return type:

float

norm_squared()[source]¶
Return type:

float

normalize()[source]¶

Return a unit-norm copy of this quaternion.

Example

>>> q = Quaternion(3, 4, 0, 0)
>>> q.normalize().norm()
1.0
Return type:

Quaternion

inverse()[source]¶

Return the multiplicative inverse q⁻¹ = q* / ‖q‖².

Example

>>> q = Quaternion(1, 0, 0, 0)
>>> q * q.inverse()
Quaternion(1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0)
Return type:

Quaternion

exp()[source]¶

Quaternion exponential.

Example

>>> q = Quaternion(0, 0.3, 0.4, 0)
>>> roundtrip = q.exp().log()
>>> abs(roundtrip.r - q.r) < 1e-10
True
Return type:

Quaternion

log()[source]¶

Quaternion logarithm.

Example

>>> q = Quaternion(0, 0.3, 0.4, 0)
>>> q.log()
Return type:

Quaternion

pow(t)[source]¶

Raise quaternion to a real power: exp(t * log(q)).

Example

>>> q = Quaternion(2, 0, 0, 0)
>>> q.pow(3)
Quaternion(8.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0)
Return type:

Quaternion

re_inner(other)[source]¶

Real inner product (dot product of coefficient vectors).

Example

>>> a = Quaternion(1, 2, 3, 4)
>>> b = Quaternion(4, 3, 2, 1)
>>> a.re_inner(b)
20.0
Parameters:

other (Quaternion)

Return type:

float

commutator(other)[source]¶

Commutator [p, q] = p*q - q*p.

Example

>>> i = Quaternion(0, 1, 0, 0)
>>> j = Quaternion(0, 0, 1, 0)
>>> i.commutator(j)
Quaternion(0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 2.0)
Parameters:

other (Quaternion)

Return type:

Quaternion

isnan()[source]¶
Return type:

bool

isinf()[source]¶
Return type:

bool

isfinite()[source]¶
Return type:

bool

isclose(other, rtol=1e-05, atol=1e-08)[source]¶
Parameters:

other (Quaternion)

Return type:

bool

rotate_vector(v)[source]¶
Return type:

ndarray

to_complex_matrix()[source]¶

2x2 complex matrix representation.

Example

>>> q = Quaternion(1, 2, 3, 4)
>>> M = q.to_complex_matrix()
>>> M.shape
(2, 2)
Return type:

ndarray

classmethod from_complex_matrix(M)[source]¶
Return type:

Quaternion

to_real_matrix_left()[source]¶

4x4 real left-multiplication matrix.

Example

>>> q = Quaternion(1, 2, 3, 4)
>>> M = q.to_real_matrix_left()
>>> M.shape
(4, 4)
Return type:

ndarray

to_real_matrix_right()[source]¶

4x4 real right-multiplication matrix.

Example

>>> q = Quaternion(1, 2, 3, 4)
>>> M = q.to_real_matrix_right()
>>> M.shape
(4, 4)
Return type:

ndarray

classmethod from_real_matrix_left(M)[source]¶
Return type:

Quaternion

property data: ndarray¶

Public accessor for the underlying (4,) ndarray (returns a copy).

to_array()[source]¶
Return type:

ndarray

to_numpy(copy=True, dtype=None)[source]¶
Parameters:

copy (bool)

Return type:

ndarray

to_json()[source]¶

Serialize to JSON string.

Example

>>> Quaternion(1,2,3,4).to_json()
'{"type": "Quaternion", "data": [1.0, 2.0, 3.0, 4.0]}'
Return type:

str

to_bytes()[source]¶

Serialize to compact binary format.

Return type:

bytes

classmethod from_json(s)[source]¶

Deserialize from JSON string.

Parameters:

s (str)

Return type:

Quaternion

classmethod from_bytes(b)[source]¶

Deserialize from binary bytes.

Parameters:

b (bytes)

Return type:

Quaternion

quat.core.quat(*args)[source]¶

Convenience constructor for Quaternion.

Return type:

Quaternion
